title: Shapeshifting
wikitext:
[[File:Tumblr_luw9nrc9bd1r2yyhzo6_250.gif|thumb|400px| [[Miss Martian (Young Justice)|Miss Martian]] shapeshifts into [[Looney Tunes (Verse)|Marvin the Martian]].[[w:c:youngjustice:Secrets|Young Justice; S1 Ep18]]; 15:12mins]] ==Summary== '''Shapeshifting''' is the power to transform and reshape the form of one's body. The user is able to freely manipulate their own bodily structure to varying degrees, and may also display other powers directly tied to their shapeshifting ability, such as [[Size Manipulation|changing size]], [[Matter Manipulation|changing the composition of their bodies]], [[Adaptation|adapting rapidly via metamorphosis to adverse conditions]], [[Additional Limbs|growing additional appendages]], [[Power Mimicry|replicating the powers of creatures and objects they turn into]], and many more. The degree of shifting possible and the array of secondary powers that come from it depend entirely on the skill of the shapeshifter and the inherent limits of their ability. Shapeshifters may have a hard time shifting their bodies beyond the smallest unit that they can control, such as a cell. They may also have a limited array of options to shift into, such as only turning into animate or inanimate objects. Shapeshifting should be distinguished from [[Transformation]], which is usually a form or set of forms that is clearly defined and can only be switched out of or into fully. In general, shapeshifters have far greater control of what they turn into, to what extent they turn into whatever it is they're turning into, and are not limited to a small predetermined number of forms. ==Users== * [[The Thing (John Carpenter)|The Thing]] ([[The Thing (Verse)|The Thing]]) * [[Astaroth (Barlowe's Hell)|Astaroth]] ([[Barlowe's Hell]]) * [[Pennywise (Books)|Pennywise]] ([[The Dark Tower]]) * [[Martian Manhunter (Post-Crisis)|Martian Manhunter]] and [[Clayface (Basil Karlo) (Post-Crisis)|Clayface]] ([[DC Comics]]) * [[Mystique (Marvel Comics)|Mystique]] ([[Marvel Comics]]) ==References==
